Hey, what's going on everyone? Welcome to the first of the five core habits of getting higher awhile life and legacy. So I want to take a quick second to give credit where credit is due. Joey Huber, who is arguably the most successful gem owner that we've had come through and he has more revenue, more net profit, he's scaled to more AFTs than anyone and not coincidentally has gone through every piece of content multiple times and all of legacy four times in total. He's an absolute executor. And so he was originally the person who put together his framework because so many people were asking him what was he doing and he's like I just took everything that Alex did about how to run my life and I started living that way. And so he and I put this together because they're more the issue of everyone who struggles with legacies is that they just aren't doing it. You know what I mean? And so they're just not executing it and for whatever reason they aren't. And so if you instill into these core habits and I'll give you the tactics to do so, you'll be phenomenally more successful. So the first one here is consistency. So if you followed any of my stuff, I talk about the boring work all the time. And the boring work is the work that is high volume and is repetitive. But the thing is, is your work works on you more than you work on it. And one of my favorite proverbs is that in all labor there's profit, not some labor, meaning even when you fail, you profit because you grow. And so looking forward to doing the repetitive work of always doing your one on ones, always, always and positivity and always speaking into your, you know, pouring into your team. Those are the boring things and they're not fun. Right? I just want to like business ownership is not always fun. All right? A lot of times it's not fun because it's a lot of boring work. And but the thing is, is the boring work is what yields unborn results. And one of the number one things I see with the people who struggle is that they have a super huge month and then they have a really small month because they get burned out. And it's being able to consistently come in day in, day out. And you're going to, if you can, if you focus on that and what you're going to, what you're going to make is your minimum amount of sales every month and slowly increase that rather than try and like hit a grand slam and then sink and do this cycle. It's not good for the business. It's not good for you. It's not for your good for your clients. It's not good for systems. It's not for the process. It's not good for anything. And so I would encourage you to try and be boring. All right? And you're going to see this thing throughout the presentation I'm going to show you, but put in the boring work. All right? And when you do that, I can promise you that you will see higher ROI out of everything you do. All right? And this is definitely an instance where consistency is going to be effort. So like if your, if your sessions, for example, are always an eight out of 10, it is better than having a 10 out of 10 session and then having a five out of 10 session because like you could probably figure that out because people will leave after five out of 10, but a 10 out of 10 is like, if you do it once and then you don't do it again, then they have a taste of something good and then it's not as good. And so it's better to be consistent than have these huge spikes in effort and the same thing for your team. Right? And so like I want everyone to shoot for consistency in terms of how they approach business and how they approach their lives. And this is obviously the theory section. I'll get into the tactics in the next video or the second half of this video. With consistency comes the compound effect, meaning the small efforts over time add up to much bigger results than intermittent efforts with lots of intensity. Right? And so this is just the classic example and I'm only using marketing campaigns because I feel like it's the thing that happens most frequently is that someone will go crazy for a marketing campaign, sell 80 challengers or something. In the next month, they'll sell 10. Right? Or they won't even market at all. And you would be so much better served in consistently getting five days a week, two sales a day. That's it. And doing that consistently and consistently converting a high percentage and consistently retaining, then you will over these huge being a hero, you're using a last-ditch effort. And that is not to be applauded. It's not good. All right? So I want you to take away from this like, yes, we did everything. Like we won the Super Bowl. It's not how it works. Like defense wins championships and like within the context of business, consistency will win every time. And you need variety because you are the entrepreneur. You want to try new shiny things. You want to like SOS shiny object syndrome is like, what is new? What's sexy? The thing is, if you keep telling your team all these new and sexy things, they stop even wanting to do the basics because like they, you were directing your team's attention. But the reality is that they need to keep doing what they need to keep doing 90% of the time. And if you have ideas, you need to keep them to yourself or tell them to someone else, but don't tell your team because they have a job to do. All right? And you need to let your business run and not constantly be changing directions all the time. All right? And I know this is difficult. And that's why I'm saying this because entrepreneurs, we want to change things. We want to shake things up. We are more rebellious by nature. But the reality is that that's not what's going to drive the business. Right? That's not what's going to make you more money. And the thing that got you from zero to one to start the business is literally everything you're going to need. You need to be stable emotionally. So this like the beginning part of this is kind of from a work standpoint, but this is from a personal standpoint, you need to be stable and you need to consistently be in the right state. So if some days you're hot, some days you're cold, I'm saying with your staff, not in terms of like your selling aptitude, but like, if you shop one day and you're like, lovey-dovey, the next next to your moody and your grumpy and the next day you're upset, the next day you're angry, and then you're nice again, like, everyone's in fear because they don't know what to expect from you. And as soon as you get into that, you have way less productive teams, they don't know what to do. And then again, your consistency drops and then the business suffers. But it's all because of you, because you weren't even able to on a basic level show up, strive to be boring, but strive to be consistent because you need to be the rock for everyone on your team so they can count on you. Because if everything else in the world is going to hell, you are the rock. It's your business. And so like, your business needs you to be this way. And if you're not like this, then you need to become like this. That I can tell you. And I mean, I don't know what, when you're watching this, but as we've continued to grow our business to levels far surpassed, that there is nothing advanced. There's nothing advanced. It's literally just never not doing the basics. And in the beginning, you might do the basics 20% of the time. And then you do the basics 25% of the time, then 30% of the time. And then over time, as you continue to do the basics, you'll start gaining momentum because things always happen. The trains always arrive on time. You always start getting referrals on a regular basis, right? Like attrition is consistently low, right? Trainers start referring their friends. So you have a steady flow of new personnel if you need them. So you can keep your quality of staff high. But the advanced stuff isn't sexy. And that's the big secret is that like, when we get when we talk to, you know, nine figure, multiple nine figure, and now we have some connections for 10 figure business owners, like they laugh when we talk about this, because they're like, I know everyone wants the secret, and there is no secret. The secret is just doing the basics. But people who consider themselves like basic, like, well, obviously, that's what you should do. But they don't actually do it. And I'm telling you, like, people just don't actually do the basics. So enough theory, let's get into tactics. So the first thing you need to do to be consistent as a human being is you need to start with the most consistent thing you can do, which is like regulating yourself from a physiological standpoint, all right, you need to be in bed for nine hours. And so that's called sleep opportunity. All right, you might not sleep for the entire nine hours, but you're going to be able to get seven to nine hours of sleep. All right. And I know this sounds like, like, beyond obvious and beyond rudimentary, but most people don't do this. Like, most gym owners do not do this, and are like, why am I impatient with my staff? Why can't I sell? Because you're exhausted, right? You don't have the capacity to be patient because you're tired. Right? Like, Harvard Business Review released a report that was like 85% of terrible management decisions happen because people are tired. That's it. It's all this, right? And so you are smarter, like, straight up smarter, actually have higher IQ when you are well rested. You are nicer, you are kinder, you are more patient, you are happier, you're leaner, you have a higher sex drive. All of these things from the simple stuff of actually consistently going to bed at the same time and getting enough sleep. I'm like, I know this sounds ridiculous, like, but people don't do it. Okay. So with consistent sleep time comes consistent wake times. All right. A lot of you guys know that I'm a big believer in waking up very early. And it's not because I, it's not because I have some obsession with very early. It's just that that's usually when Moan's going to bother me, right? And probably when most people aren't going to bother you. And so I get my most important things done in the first three to four hours of my day. Those are going to be my highest ROI activities. That's my highest ROI time. I'm the most rested. I don't have anything that's come into my, my world that might annoy me or stress me out. The legacy portal is this is when you need to do it. Okay. You wake up, you know, you'll do the, we'll give you the planner, you'll go through your planner and you'll just identify those. It'll take five minutes, right? But that five minutes will center your attention, center you in such a way that you'll get more out of the subsequent day. All right. So you do your big things first. And right now, if you have not gone through all of legacy, I am, I'm, I'm asking you to trust me, which is a big ask. I know there is no higher return on your time and attention than going through every one of these videos that we have and doing them in sequence and executing the things in there. And believe it or not, going through more than once, all of the videos, because you'll get more and more each time. Because you, you can't even have, you don't have the capacity, no one does, to get everything when you go through something one time. All right. So this is like your primary homework on a daily basis that you do that you invest in yourself, in your business. So you're going to go to sleep at the same time, and you're going to wake up at the same time, and you're going to wake up early. Now you're like, hold on, how am I going to get eight hours of sleep if I'm waking up at four? Like, or let's say five, how are you going to do that? You're asleep by nine. How do you do that? You get into bed by eight and 30. Wait, what? Yeah. If you want different outcomes, you need to live differently. One of the other things that I see is you need to have the same time on the weekends. Okay. So here's my bad diet analogy. You always have these clients who are good during the week, five days a week, and then they, and they throw it all the shit on the weekends, right? From this point going forward, if you want to be a top gym owner and a top business owner, your weekends and your weekdays are the same. They are no different. They are all just days in the week and they're days of how you live your life. If you look forward to the weekend, you're doing something wrong. Your days should be the same. If you want to take a day off, sure. But your sleep cycle, your wake cycle, and what you do to start each of those days should still be the same. So if you wake up at five a.m. every day during the week, you should wake up at five a.m. during the weekends. If you go to sleep at eight thirty during the week, then you should go to sleep at eight thirty during the weekends. But wait, what does that mean? Like, my friends want to go out. Then you need to find a different set of friends or you need to tell your friends and then they will adjust. If your friends don't want to see you unless you want to go out late, then you might not have the best friends. Okay? Being really real with you. You need to decide whether your goals are important enough for you to sacrifice other things. And I'm telling you, this will go a very long way for you. That's a promise. Okay? Keep the changes in your day to day minimal. The reason for that is that you get micro decision fatigue. Alright? So you have to make lots of decisions as a business owner and you want the most processing horsepower available to you at any given moment so that you can make the best decisions. The last thing you want to do is waste horsepower on what shirt you're going to wear, what lunch you're going to have, what pants you're going to wear, you know, whatever. Right? And so we do lots of things to decrease the noise in our lives and that you should decrease the noise in your life by doing so eating the same things for breakfast, lunch and dinner. It sounds really simple, but from a physiological standpoint, it's actually something that it'll elongate your life. It decreases gastric stress, which is real stress, and you're like your body interprets stress from physical and emotional in the same way. So your cortisol levels go lower, your digestion gets easier. It's easier for you to manipulate your body fat because once you have a consistent basis, all you need to do is to start knocking things off if you want to, but you just eat more or less of the same things. But this will give you so much structure in your life, you always have the same groceries, which means you can put it on into the cart and you can have it shipped to your door. You have the same things that come from Amazon, install the buttons so that you don't have to think about it. And then all of a sudden, what do we just do? We just got all of this time back because your groceries are delivered immediately. Whenever things are getting closer running out, they appear two days later because you hit the Amazon button, you wear the same shirts. So in the morning, you're not thinking, you just grab, grab, you put it on and you go straight to work. This is how you breed consistency in your life from a personal standpoint and from a business standpoint. It makes you less reactive, which makes you more potent. And the last thing here is that you need a journal to focus your attention. And so we provided the gym Lord's Journal, which is kind of the consolidation of as many journals as Layla and I have gone through and hit on these key habits just by asking a few of the questions. You only need to put one word answers on, very simple. I'll explain more when we get to that. But it will focus you and then it will center you, will make you less reactive. And if you can do just this, just this and for every time you're like, well, I don't have four minutes, everybody has four minutes, put it next to your toilet for all I care. Like, but just do it. Because consistency is what's going to be the brick, the cornerstone of all of the things that we build on top of this. So these are the tactics to be consistent. Go to bed at the same time, wake up at the same time, weekends and weekdays are the same. Even with your out with friends, whatever, do it earlier, don't do it. You're going to throw yourself off. And the more ingredient you become in this routine, the harder it is to switch it. So my ask for you is, please do it. Okay. And keep your decisions that you need to make that on a regular basis as as to as as minimal as possible. All right, the things that you always do every day that you need to decide, stop deciding them, decide ahead of time and then just live that way. Cool. Good talk. We'll get to the next one.
